findings.evidence: Roadmap README.md line 187: '⚠ A naïve `IsUnipotent` that tests nilpotence in the reduced ring `A` is *vacuous* (only `g = 1` qualifies): the correct definition needs comodule theory (Layer 1).'
findings.fix: Add `@[experimental]` (or mark `private`) so that the Layer 1 replacement is not a breaking change and no `@[deprecated]` alias is required.
findings.issue: `IsUnipotent` uses the naïve criterion `IsNilpotent ((g : A) - 1)` that the roadmap (README.md:187–188) explicitly labels 'vacuous — only g = 1 qualifies' and flags as requiring replacement once comodule theory (Layer 1) exists. As an unqualified public `def`, replacing it with the comodule-theoretic definition is an undeprecated breaking API change.

findings.evidence: Roadmap README.md lines 21–28: 'Keep two distinct notions, and never make smoothness implicit … an affine group scheme of finite type … [admits] μ_p, αₚ, and other non-smooth/non-reduced groups … add smoothness as a named hypothesis.'
findings.fix: Either mark `AffineAlgGroup` `@[experimental]`, or split it now into `AffineGroupScheme` (finite-type only) and let `AffineAlgGroup` extend with smoothness — matching the roadmap's explicit design requirement.
findings.issue: `AffineAlgGroup` bundles `Algebra.FiniteType k A` and `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced k A` into one class. The roadmap (README.md:21–28) explicitly requires keeping these separate so that non-smooth groups (`μ_p`, `αₚ`) can be handled at finite-type generality. When future layers introduce a non-smooth base class, `AffineAlgGroup` must be weakened or split — an undeprecated breaking change on a public `class`.

summary: Two public declarations will require incompatible API changes as the roadmap layers build out, but neither is marked experimental or internal — so replacing them later silently breaks any downstream code. Fix: mark both experimental now so no deprecation alias is owed when they are superseded.
